jokes.text: ¶ eminem is not very good ¶ ¶ 50 cent is not very good ¶ ¶ Snoop dogg is not very good ¶ ¶ mos def is not very good ¶ ¶ but the rhymenoceros is very very good... ¶
jokes.setup: Bret systematically dissing famous rappers in his rap
jokes.punchline: Declares himself ('rhymenoceros') superior to all established rap legends
jokes.explanation: Strong character comedy - perfectly captures Bret's delusional confidence. 'Rhymenoceros' is genuinely clever wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: eminem is not very good

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: 50 cent is not very good

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Snoop dogg is not very good

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: mos def is not very good

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: but the rhymenoceros is very very good...

jokes.text: bass solo. Bass solo over.
jokes.setup: Epic bass solo announcement in middle of rap
jokes.punchline: Immediate anticlimatic declaration that it's over
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. The deadpan delivery makes the anticlimax funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: bass solo. Bass solo over.

jokes.text: Greg's written this. He's put the rs too far apart.
jokes.setup: Murray looking at something Greg wrote
jokes.punchline: Complaint about letter spacing rather than content
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Murray's petty managerial focus on trivial issues.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Greg's written this. He's put the rs too far apart.

jokes.text: Texas, Lexus... two rhyming words. Do you think you could use those?
jokes.punchline: Suggests random unconnected rhyming words as if that's songwriting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Murray's clueless enthusiasm and complete misunderstanding of creativity.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Texas, Lexus... two rhyming words. Do you think you could use those?

jokes.text: You can make sound, just make it so that people can't hear you.
jokes.setup: Band complaining they can't play loudly in library
jokes.punchline: Murray's illogical solution to make sound that's unhearable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic delivered with complete sincerity. Highly quotable nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You can make sound, just make it so that people can't hear you.

jokes.text: No, it was Snoop dogg. - I know he's a dog, Bret.
jokes.punchline: Murray thinks Bret dissed the cartoon dog Snoopy
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Murray's cultural disconnect and stubborn confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No, it was Snoop dogg.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I know he's a dog, Bret.

jokes.text: He's lovable. Leave him alone.
jokes.punchline: Sincere protection of a cartoon character from rap beef
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Murray's misunderstanding with genuine emotional investment.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: He's lovable. Leave him alone.

jokes.text: All year.
jokes.setup: Murray asking how long Greg has been hearing him
jokes.punchline: Devastating simple answer revealing scope of embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that devastate Murray. Great deadpan delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Greg
dialogue.line: All year.

jokes.text: What's wrong with that guy? Useless. What does he do all day? You didn't hear that, did you, greg? Good, so he didn't hear that last bit.
jokes.setup: Murray complaining about Greg while Greg is listening
jokes.punchline: Murray thinks Greg didn't hear his insult when Greg clearly did
jokes.explanation: Extends the intercom gag effectively, showing Murray's obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: What's wrong with that guy? Useless. What does he do all day?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You didn't hear that, did you, greg?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Good, so he didn't hear that last bit.

jokes.text: That's how you diss someone. Did you see what I did there? I didn't start a fight with greg, I didn't hurt his feelings. Greg's got feelings.
jokes.punchline: Claiming he protected Greg's feelings while openly insulting him
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Murray's complete lack of self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: That's how you diss someone. Did you see what I did there? I didn't start a fight with greg, I didn't hurt his feelings. Greg's got feelings.

jokes.text: ¶ some people say that rappers don't have feelings ¶ ¶ we have feelings ¶
jokes.setup: Song premise about rappers having feelings
jokes.punchline: Overly sincere declaration of having feelings
jokes.explanation: Great song concept that perfectly fits the show's style of sincere parody.

dialogue.line: some people say that rappers don't have feelings
we have feelings

jokes.text: ¶ we're vincible ¶
jokes.punchline: Claiming to be 'vincible' - vulnerable to being conquered
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both funny and reveals character vulnerability. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: we're vincible

jokes.text: ¶ I feel like a prize asshole, no one even mentions my casserole ¶
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'asshole' with 'casserole' while expressing genuine hurt
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of crude humor and sincere emotion. The rhyme is unexpected and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I feel like a prize asshole, no one even mentions my casserole

jokes.text: ¶ you could've said something nice about my profita rolls ¶

jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Bret's attempt at sophistication gone wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: you could've said something nice about my profita rolls

jokes.text: ¶ and the assistant suggested I try a ladies' size ¶

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: and the assistant suggested I try a ladies' size

jokes.text: ¶ I'm not gonna wear a ladies' wetsuit, I'm a man ¶
jokes.punchline: Overly defensive assertion of masculinity
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Jemaine's wounded pride.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I'm not gonna wear a ladies' wetsuit, I'm a man

jokes.text: ¶ get me a small man's wetsuit, please ¶
jokes.punchline: Awkward specific request that undermines his masculinity protest
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline that undercuts his masculine protest with specific awkward request.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: get me a small man's wetsuit, please

jokes.text: ¶ they forgot about me ¶
jokes.setup: Waiting for birthday call from family
jokes.punchline: Simple, devastatingly sad statement
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical expression of genuine sadness that's funny through understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: they forgot about me

jokes.text: ¶ the day after my birthday is not my birthday, mom ¶
jokes.punchline: Obvious statement delivered as profound complaint
jokes.explanation: Great line that captures hurt through stating the obvious with genuine emotion.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: the day after my birthday is not my birthday, mom

jokes.setup: Finding friends who said they were too busy

jokes.explanation: Specific movie choice adds to the betrayal but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: they're all lined up to watch that movie 'maid in manhattan'

jokes.text: ¶ they're all lined up to watch that movie 'maid in Manhattan' ¶
jokes.setup: Friends excluding him from group activity
jokes.punchline: Specific mundane rom-com they chose over hanging with him
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that adds to the hurt feelings narrative

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: they're all lined up to watch that movie 'maid in Manhattan'

jokes.text: ¶ once again, they forgot about j ¶

dialogue.line: once again, they forgot about j

jokes.text: ¶ were you ever called 'homo' because at school you took Drama? ¶
jokes.punchline: Very specific, personal question about taking drama class
jokes.explanation: Good character detail revealing Bret's specific insecurities about his artistic interests.

dialogue.line: were you ever called 'homo' because at school you took Drama?

jokes.text: ¶ have you ever been told that you look like a llama? ¶
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Random but memorable animal comparison that suggests another personal insecurity.

dialogue.line: have you ever been told that you look like a llama?

jokes.text: Death? - Yeah, or worse. Isn't it the same way with rappers back in newfoundland? - I'm not sure. - New zealand.
jokes.setup: Murray warning about rapper rivalries resulting in 'death or worse'
jokes.punchline: Bret casually correcting Murray's geography mistake
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about confusion over New Zealand geography during serious conversation.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Death?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yeah, or worse. Isn't it the same way with rappers back in newfoundland?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I'm not sure.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: New zealand.

jokes.text: I heard of one rapper... he chopped this guy's whole body off, just left the dick behind. Don't you mean they... they chopped off his dick? No, I mean they held his dick and chopped his whole body off. That's all he was in the end... a dick.
jokes.punchline: Absurd logistics of chopping off entire body while holding onto dick, ending with unintended pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that builds to an unintended but perfect pun. Murray's earnest delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I heard of one rapper... he chopped this guy's whole body off, just left the dick behind.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Don't you mean they... they chopped off his dick?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: No, I mean they held his dick and chopped his whole body off. That's all he was in the end... a dick.

jokes.text: Do I have gang experience? I was in the snake eyes, the duckies, the cuckoos, the vampires, the pharaohs, the ballroom dancers, the hobo lords, the little enchanters...
jokes.setup: Bret asking about Dave's gang experience
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous list of gang names including 'ballroom dancers' and 'little enchanters'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from tough-sounding to absurd gang names. 'Little enchanters' is particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Do I have gang experience? I was in the snake eyes, the duckies, the cuckoos, the vampires, the pharaohs, the ballroom dancers, the hobo lords, the little enchanters...

jokes.text: I was a navy seal for 15 years. - Okay. - I'm officially awol, so don't ask them about my existence, 'cause they'll deny it.
jokes.punchline: Paranoid claim about being AWOL navy seal whose existence is denied
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Dave's paranoid delusions mixed with genuine belief.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I was a navy seal for 15 years.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I'm officially awol, so don't ask them about my existence, 'cause they'll deny it.

jokes.text: He says you have a kind face.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Li explaining why they want to join gang
jokes.punchline: Husband's reason is Bret has a kind face (opposite of intimidating)
jokes.explanation: Perfect undermining of Bret's tough guy aspirations through innocent observation.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Li
dialogue.line: He says you have a kind face.

jokes.text: watering can tied to a hose. Pop! Cops show up, 'what's that, officer? No, just watering the geraniums.'
jokes.punchline: Elaborate scenario of disguising murder weapon as gardening tool
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dave's bizarre but detailed paranoid thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: watering can tied to a hose. Pop! Cops show up, 'what's that, officer? No, just watering the geraniums.'

jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor that plays on Murray's bureaucratic role and gullibility.

jokes.text: Just pulling your tit. Yeah, just pulling your boob.

Yeah, just pulling your boob.

jokes.text: This gang is disbandoned. - No. Dis... what? - Disbandoned. - Disbanded you mean? - Abandoned? Yup, all of those things.

jokes.punchline: Murray's malapropism 'disbandoned' and doubling down when corrected
jokes.explanation: Great malapropism that Murray commits to, showing his stubborn pride when corrected.

dialogue.line: This gang is disbandoned.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No. Dis... what?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Disbandoned.

dialogue.speaker: Bret

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Abandoned? Yup, all of those things.

jokes.text: Yes. Who was in your gang, you and your mom? No, Jemaine. It was me, my brother graeme, and my dad Gordon. Mom wasn't even allowed in it. No one knew why.
jokes.punchline: Murray's earnest description of family bike gang with mysterious mom exclusion
jokes.explanation: Great character backstory that's both innocent and mysteriously dysfunctional.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yes. Who was in your gang, you and your mom?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: No, Jemaine. It was me, my brother graeme, and my dad Gordon. Mom wasn't even allowed in it. No one knew why.

jokes.text: Dad was the leader, graeme found nice places for us to ride to and I was the guy that looked after the bags.
jokes.punchline: Murray's role was the least tough - bag carrier
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail showing Murray's position in family hierarchy.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Dad was the leader, graeme found nice places for us to ride to and I was the guy that looked after the bags.

jokes.text: I had to throw it away! Because Bret was jealous. Really? Was he going apeshit? He went apeshit.
jokes.setup: Jemaine explaining why he discarded the painting
jokes.punchline: Making up story about Bret's jealousy to spare Mel's feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jemaine's awkward attempt at kindness through deception.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I had to throw it away! Because Bret was jealous.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Really? Was he going apeshit?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: He went apeshit.

jokes.text: I swear on Doug's life I will fix this rift.
jokes.punchline: Swearing on her boyfriend's life for minor issue
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mel's dramatic overreaction to minor issues

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: I swear on Doug's life I will fix this rift.

dialogue.line: Miss new zealand... best looking Sheila in the whole country. Sorry, I didn't recognize you without the crown and sash.

dialogue.speaker: Ambassador
dialogue.line: But I thought flossy the sheep won miss new zealand every year.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: have you ever been told that you're miss new zealand?

dialogue.speaker: Bret

jokes.text: Where's the dapper dukes' hideout? It was right here. You know, we laid low for three straight months back in '58.

jokes.punchline: Specific details about 1950s gang with classy name 'dapper dukes'
jokes.explanation: 'Dapper dukes' is a great gang name that fits Dave's delusional nostalgia perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Where's the dapper dukes' hideout? It was right here. You know, we laid low for three straight months back in '58.

jokes.text: Maybe you're right, lil Louie. I'm getting old. This game ain't no good for me no more. I'm sorry, Louie. I'm out. So long, lil Louie.
jokes.setup: Dave having emotional breakdown about gang life
jokes.punchline: Calling Bret 'lil Louie' and dramatic exit from toy gang
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic exit that treats the silly gang with noir movie seriousness.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Maybe you're right, lil Louie. I'm getting old. This game ain't no good for me no more. I'm sorry, Louie. I'm out. So long, lil Louie.

jokes.text: How come I'm wearing gumboots?
jokes.setup: Bret looking at Mel's painting of him

jokes.explanation: Good specific detail question that undermines the heroic portrait concept.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: How come I'm wearing gumboots?

jokes.text: Well, in the future, I suggest you don't kick children. we'll let this go because we don't like that particular kid, but we've got our eyes on you.
jokes.punchline: Police admitting they also dislike the specific kid but still warning Bret
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of authority - police being unprofessional in perfectly professional tone.

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: Well, in the future, I suggest you don't kick children. we'll let this go because we don't like that particular kid, but we've got our eyes on you.

jokes.text: As you can see, I pretty much got the handsomeness. That's Jemaine. If you don't believe it, he can take his shirt off and you can be sure about it.

jokes.explanation: Perfect Mel moment showing her complete lack of appropriate boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: As you can see, I pretty much got the handsomeness. That's Jemaine. If you don't believe it, he can take his shirt off and you can be sure about it.
